entries.definition:
كتاب الحاء

حبس

(حبل) الحاء والباء واللام أصلٌ واحدٌ يدلُّ على امتداد الشيء. ثمّ يحمل عليه، ومَرْجِع الفروع مرجعٌ واحد. فالحبل الرَّسَن، معروف، والجمع حِبال. والحبل: حبل العاتق. والحَبل: القطعة من الرّمل يستطيل.

ḥ–b–l rope, halter; connection, link, means, covenant, pledge; snare, wiles, stratagem; to conceive a child. Of this root, two words occur seven times in the Qurʾan: حَبْل ḥabl five times and حِبَال ḥibāl twice.
حَبْل ḥabl [n.; pl. حِبَال ḥibāl] rope, cord (26:44) فَأَلْقَوْا۟ حِبَالَهُمْ وَعِصِيَّهُمْ and they threw their ropes and staffs, and said: ‘By Pharaoh’s might, we shall be the victors’; *(3:103) بِحَبْلِ ٱللَّهِ bond, pledge, covenant of God [lit. by the rope of God]; *(3:112) حَبْلٍ مِّنَ ٱلنَّاسِ assistance from mankind; *(50:16) حَبْل ٱلْوَرِيدِ the jugular vein.

حَتَّىٰ ḥattā particle occurring 142 times in the Qurʾan and broadly functioning as: I preposition, meaning ‘until’, ‘to the point of’, ‘up to’, ‘as far as’. It precedes either a noun, as in (97:5) حَتَّىٰ مَطْلَعِ ٱلْفَجْرِ till the rising of dawn; or a sentence, as in (20:91) حَتَّىٰ يَرْجِعَ إِلَيْنَا مُوسَىٰ until Moses returns to us II conjunction 1 meaning ‘in order that’, ‘in order to’, as in (63:7) هُمُ ٱلَّذِينَ يَقُولُونَ لَا تُنفِقُوا۟ عَلَىٰ مَنْ عِندَ رَسُولِ ٱللَّهِ حَتَّىٰ يَنفَضُّوا۟ they are the ones who say, ‘Do not give financial support to those who follow God’s Messenger, so that they may disperse.’ Many instances of the conjunctive, meaning ‘in order to’ are also interpreted as prepositional, meaning ‘until’, as in (49:9) فَقَٰتِلُوا۟ ٱلَّتِى تَبْغِى حَتَّىٰ تَفِىٓءَ إِلَىٰٓ أَمْرِ ٱللَّهِ fight that which does wrong until (or, in order that) it returns to God’s commandment 2 meaning ‘unless’ (8:53) ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَمْ يَكُ مُغَيِّرًا نِّعْمَةً أَنْعَمَهَا عَلَىٰ قَوْمٍ حَتَّىٰ يُغَيِّرُوا۟ مَا بِأَنفُسِهِمْ that is because God would never change a favour He had conferred upon a people unless they change what is in themselves III said to be synonymous with the meaning of exceptive إِلَّا, possibly as in (2:102) وَمَا يُعَلِّمَانِ مِنْ أَحَدٍ حَتَّىٰ يَقُولَآ إِنَّمَا نَحْنُ فِتْنَةٌ yet they [these two] never taught anyone before/without first saying, ‘We are a test, so do not [fall for us and] reject the faith.’
